input.headline: NSW school endures floods, fires and an angry camel to mark 180 years

raw.headline: NSW school endures floods, fires and an angry camel to mark 180 years

winner: The principal said it was their worst year yet—floods, fires, and a rogue camel—but at least they didn’t have to deal with any *dromedary drama*.

candidates.text: The principal tried explaining the school's rough year as part of history—floods, fires, and that camel incident—but the kids kept interrupting with, "Wait, is the camel in our curriculum?
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist with the camel in curriculum, concise and specific to headline, natural humor"}

candidates.text: The school's anniversary party was a disaster — the cake was eaten by a flood, the candles were blown out by fire, and the guest of honor was a very disgruntled camel.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Creative and specific, with a surprising twist involving the camel; concise and funny without being offensive."}

candidates.text: After 180 years, the NSW school finally has a complete set of disasters — floods, fires, and one very disgruntled camel.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising and specific twist with the angry camel, concise and naturally funny."}

candidates.text: The principal said it was their worst year yet—floods, fires, and a rogue camel—but at least they didn’t have to deal with any *dromedary drama*.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Puns on 'dromedary drama' add a clever twist, aligning with the camel reference while keeping the joke concise and surprising."}

candidates.text: The school's anniversary celebration was quite the spectacle—floods, fires, and a very disgruntled camel all making sure nobody forgot the 180th birthday.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising and specific combination of events with a touch of humor in 'disgruntled camel'."}

candidates.text: The school's 180th anniversary was truly a humping milestone for the students.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Puns on 'humping' tie in with the camel reference, concise and surprising."}

candidates.text: The school’s 180th anniversary celebration was a triumph of survival, proving that while the students were terrified of the floods, fires, and angry camels, the camels were actually just trying to evacuate the building before the ceremony started.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist with camels trying to evacuate adds humor; concise and specific to the headline."}

candidates.text: The school's 180th anniversary was so uneventful that even the camel refused to make a scene—it just stood there, judging everyone silently.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat surprising and specific, but the setup contrasts with the headline in a way that feels slightly forced. The camel's 'judging' adds a mild humorous twist, but it lacks the punchiness and direct connection to the headline's dramatic events."}

candidates.text: The school board decided to let the camel lead the anniversary tour, but the administration had to veto the plan after it insisted on a "dry" ceremony and a "fire-free" itinerary.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.0, "reason": "Puns on 'dry' and 'fire-free' are clever and specific to the headline's context, showing good surprise and natural humor."}
